function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for everything that finishes.
Background: ONLY for long-running servers, watchers, or processes that never exit. Set background=true to get a session_id, then use process(action="wait") to block until done — it returns instantly on completion, same as foreground. Use process(action="poll") only when you need a progress check without blocking.
Do NOT use background for scripts, builds, or installs — foreground with a generous timeout is always better (fewer tool calls, instant results).
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.
Important: cloud sandboxes may be cleaned up, idled out, or recreated between turns. Persistent filesystem means files can resume later; it does NOT guarantee a continuously running machine or surviving background processes. Use terminal sandboxes for task work, not durable hosting.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: ONLY for servers/watchers that never exit. For scripts, builds, installs — use foreground with timeout instead (it returns instantly when done).

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

check_interval.description: Seconds between automatic status checks for background processes (gateway/messaging only, minimum 30). When set, I'll proactively report progress.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

Here is the complete gap analysis of the Fun4Me Store codebase:

== GAP ANALYSIS: Fun4Me Store ==
Audited 65 source files across src/app, src/components, src/lib, src/types

=== 1) BROKEN IMPORTS / MISSING DEPENDENCIES ===

- ProductCard receives product data with `images` array from Supabase but the component interface expects `image_url` (string). The homepage/category/search pages spread the full product object but ProductCard never reads `images[0]` -- it looks for `image_url` which doesn't exist on the Supabase row. Result: all product cards fall back to placeholder images.

- `kink_categories` and `product_kinks` tables are NOT in the Database type (database.ts). All access uses `supabase as any` with eslint-disable comments. This is fragile and will break if Supabase types are regenerated.

- The `experience_level` column filter in categoria/[slug]/page.tsx uses `'experience_level' as string` type cast, confirming the DB types don't include extended columns.

- No API routes exist (/app/api is absent). The checkout form calls no API -- orders are stored in sessionStorage only, NOT in Supabase.

=== 2) PLACEHOLDER / HARDCODED DATA ===

- CATEGORY_IMAGES, KINK_IMAGES, PRODUCT_PLACEHOLDERS, HERO_IMAGE all use Unsplash stock photos. These are generic photos (abstract art, water drops) unrelated to actual products.

- BANK_DETAILS in checkout (account "[REDACTED_PHONE]", RUC "[REDACTED_PHONE]") are placeholder/dummy values.

- FREE_SHIPPING_THRESHOLD is hardcoded differently: 300,000 in carrito and checkout, 500,000 in cart-drawer. Inconsistent.

- SHIPPING_ZONES costs are hardcoded in checkout.tsx (15K, 25K, 40K). Should come from config/DB.

- WHATSAPP_NUMBER has masked digits: '+595****9739' in constants.ts but footer shows full number. Inconsistency.

- The `formatPrice` function is duplicated: exists in lib/utils/format.ts but is re-declared locally in 3 admin pages (dashboard, pedidos, pedido detail, productos). Should use the shared one.

- AnnouncementBar text "4.9 Google" is hardcoded.

=== 3) INCOMPLETE / STUB PAGES ===

- LOGIN PAGE: Completely non-functional. The form has no onSubmit handler, no Supabase auth integration, no error handling, no state management. Just renders a static HTML form.

- ADMIN LAYOUT: No authentication guard. Anyone can access /admin/* routes. The middleware explicitly skips /admin and /login paths.

- CHECKOUT: Does NOT persist orders to Supabase. Only stores in sessionStorage. No actual order creation. Receipt file upload has a file picker but the file is never uploaded anywhere.

- NEWSLETTER: Just shows a toast. No actual email collection (no API call, no Supabase insert).

- FOOTER "Ayuda" links: All 4 links (Envios, Devolucion, FAQ, Privacidad) are non-interactive spans with cursor-default. No actual pages exist for them.

- OFERTAS nav link in header: Points to `/#ofertas` (homepage section) instead of `/ofertas` (the actual offers page).

=== 4) MISSING ERROR HANDLING ===

- NO error.tsx boundary files exist anywhere in the app tree. If any server component throws, users see an unhandled Next.js error page.

- NO not-found.tsx files exist. The default Next.js 404 page renders (in English, not Spanish).

- Homepage: Supabase queries have no error handling. If DB is down, page silently renders empty arrays.

- Category/Product/Kink pages: Only handle "not found" for missing items but not database connection errors.

- Header component: Client-side Supabase fetch in useEffect has no error handling. If it fails, categories/kinks silently remain empty arrays.

- ProductForm: Try/catch exists but uses `any` for error type.

- OrderStatusUpdate: Uses `any` for error type.

- Checkout handleSubmit: Catch block catches but only sets a generic error message.

=== 5) MISSING LOADING STATES ===

- NO loading.tsx files exist anywhere. During server component data fetching, there's no loading skeleton or spinner.

- Homepage: No loading state while fetching categories, kinks, featured products.

- Category page: No loading state while products load.

- Product detail page: No loading state.

- Admin dashboard/orders/products: No loading states.

- Search page: No loading indicator while server-side search runs.

- Header: Categories load client-side with no loading indicator; nav shows empty until fetch completes.

- Cart page is client-only (good, no loading needed) but has no SSR hydration guard -- could flash empty state.

=== 6) ACCESSIBILITY ISSUES ===

- Cart quantity buttons in carrito/page.tsx lack aria-labels. Screen readers see empty buttons.

- Cart drawer quantity buttons (+/-/delete) have no aria-labels.

- ProductCard: "Add to cart" button inside a Link creates nested interactive elements (button inside anchor) -- a major a11y violation.

- Category filter buttons in filters.tsx have no aria-pressed or role="radio" attributes.

- Shipping zone buttons in checkout have no aria-pressed attribute.

- Payment method buttons in checkout have no aria-selected attribute.

- ProductTabs: Tab buttons lack role="tab", aria-selected, aria-controls attributes. Tab panels lack role="tabpanel".

- Age verification inputs lack proper label association (uses raw <label> instead of Label component, no htmlFor on some).

- Mobile menu hamburger button: has aria-label but no aria-expanded attribute.

- Categories dropdown in header: no aria-haspopup or aria-expanded.

- Progress bar in cart has no role="progressbar" or aria-valuenow.

- Admin sidebar nav links lack aria-current="page".

- Order status select in admin uses raw <select> without accessible label association.

=== 7) MISSING MOBILE RESPONSIVENESS ===

- Admin layout sidebar is `hidden md:block` but there's NO mobile hamburger menu for admin. On mobile, the admin sidebar is completely inaccessible.

- Admin tables: Orders and Products tables don't have horizontal scroll containers. On narrow screens, columns overflow.

- Product detail trust badges grid (2 cols) may be too tight on very small screens.

- Cart page item layout is `flex-col sm:flex-row` -- good, but the quantity controls could be cramped on very small devices.

- Checkout shipping zone grid is `sm:grid-cols-3` which works, but the zone buttons may be too small on some devices.

- Overall mobile support is good due to Tailwind responsive classes, but admin panel is notably lacking.

=== 8) CART STORE FUNCTIONALITY GAPS ===

- Cart items don't store product images. The CartItem type has image_url but ProductActions doesn't pass it. ProductCard's handleAddToCart also doesn't pass it. Result: cart drawer and cart page show a generic gradient box instead of product images.

- No "add with quantity" -- addItem always adds 1. No way to add multiple at once from product page.

- totalItems and totalPrice are computed functions (not selectors). This works but means they re-compute on every render, not cached.

- No coupon/discount code support in cart.

- No "save for later" / wishlist feature.

- No notification when item already in cart (just silently increments quantity).

- max_stock enforcement: addItem respects max_stock when incrementing, but updateQuantity does NOT check max_stock. Users can type any quantity.

- Cart persistence uses localStorage via zustand persist. On SSR, this causes hydration mismatch. The cart-drawer and carrito pages have no hydration guard (no mounted state check), though checkout page does have one.

=== 9) CHECKOUT FLOW COMPLETENESS ===

- CRITICAL: Orders are NOT saved to Supabase. handleSubmit generates a fake order number (timestamp-based), stores order in sessionStorage, clears cart, and redirects. No database persistence at all.

- Receipt file upload is UI-only. The File object is captured in state but never uploaded to Supabase Storage or any backend.

- No email/SMS notification system.

- No order tracking capability -- order numbers are generated client-side with Date.now() and are not queryable.

- No stock validation at checkout time. User could order more than available stock.

- No duplicate order prevention.

- No email field in checkout form (only name, phone, address).

- Phone validation regex requires starting with 0 (local format) but WhatsApp number uses international format. Could confuse users.

- Confirmation page depends entirely on sessionStorage -- if user refreshes, the order data is lost.

- No payment gateway integration (by design, WhatsApp/transfer based).

=== 10) ADMIN PANEL COMPLETENESS ===

- NO AUTHENTICATION: Admin routes have zero auth protection. The middleware skips /admin entirely. The login page is a non-functional stub. Anyone with the URL can access the full admin panel.

- Dashboard: Functional but uses `any` types extensively. Shows today's orders, revenue, low stock, recent orders.

- Products list: Works. Links to /admin/productos/nuevo which uses the same [id] route with id="nuevo" -- clever but fragile.

- Product form: Functional for create/update/delete. Uses client-side Supabase writes directly (no API layer, no server actions). This means RLS policies must allow anonymous writes, which is a security concern.

- Orders list: Works with status filtering.

- Order detail: Works with inline status update via client-side Supabase.

- MISSING admin features:
  * No category management (CRUD for categories)
  * No kink category management
  * No image upload -- product images must be pasted as URLs manually
  * No bulk operations (bulk delete, bulk stock update)
  * No search/filter on products list
  * No pagination on products or orders lists (loads ALL records)
  * No analytics/reports beyond today's stats
  * No user/customer management
  * No settings page
  * No export functionality (CSV/PDF)
  * No order creation from admin
  * No audit log
  * Mobile admin is completely broken (sidebar hidden, no alternative)

=== SUMMARY OF CRITICAL ISSUES ===

1. Orders never reach Supabase -- checkout is a dead end
2. Admin panel is completely unprotected -- no auth
3. Login page is a non-functional stub
4. Product images don't flow to product cards (image_url vs images mismatch)
5. No error boundaries or loading states anywhere
6. Free shipping threshold is inconsistent (300K vs 500K)
7. formatPrice duplicated in 3 admin files
8. Cart hydration issues (no mounted guard in drawer/cart page)
9. Nested interactive elements (button inside Link) in ProductCard
10. Admin panel is inaccessible on mobile
